title: Computer implemented method of and optimisation tool for refinement of laser cutting process parameters by means of an optimization tool
abstract: In one aspect the invention relates to a method of calculating process parameters, which are optimized for processing a workpiece with specific material properties by means of a laser machine, comprising the method steps of: Determining (S10) material properties for which the process parameters should be optimized; Determining (S20) preconfigured initial process parameters; Executing a re-optimization algorithm (S30) until a target objective function is minimized or maximized for calculating optimized material-specific process parameters by accessing a storage with a statistical model, wherein the statistical model is based on Bayesian optimization using Gaussian Processes as priors.
claims:
Claims 33 Patent Claims 1 . Computer-implemented method for calculating process parameters, being laser cutting parameters, which are machine- and material-specifically optimized for cutting a workpiece with specific material properties by means of a laser machine (L) with specific machine properties, comprising the method steps of: - Determining (S10) material and/or machine properties for which the process parameters should be optimized; - Determining (S20) preconfigured initial process parameters; - Re-defining (S33) a parameter domain for a re-optimization algorithm based on the determined preferences; - Executing the re-optimization algorithm (S30) until a target objective function is optimized for calculating material-specific process parameters by accessing a storage (S) with a statistical model, wherein execution of the re-optimization algorithm (S30) comprises: - Controlling execution (S31 ) of test processes with the determined preconfigured initial process parameters; - Evaluating (S32) the test processes by using a measurement-based algorithm or a preference-based algorithm for determining preferences for the test processes. 2. Method according to claim 1 , wherein the calculated optimized material-specific process parameters (PPMP) are directly used for controlling (S35) the laser process. 3. Method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the measurement-based algorithm is based on Bayesian Optimization, BO, using Gaussian Processes, GP. 4. Method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the preference-based algorithm is based on a pairwise comparison algorithm by comparing each test processing result with the best achieved one and wherein preferences are implemented as constraints. 5. Method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the material properties at least comprise a material type dataset (ty-ds) and/or a thickness dataset (th-ds). 

 34 6. Method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein evaluating (S32) the test processes is based on a standardized quality measure comprising a set of quality indices, which preferably consists of slag residue, kerf width, burr height, perpendicularity of cutting edge, roughness, and/or robustness of the laser process, in particular in dependence of a feed rate of the laser head. . Method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein determining (S20) the preconfigured initial process parameters is performed by selecting from a set of process parameters those parameters, which are determined to be optimal for the same or for similar material properties as the determined material properties. . Method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein for re-defining the parameter domain (S33) a physical model of the laser process is used. . Method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein evaluating (S32) the test processes a standardized quality metric is determined for different types of sensor data, including in particular optical sensor data by means of using a set of optical sensors and/or diodes. 10. Method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the re-optimization algorithm (S30) optimizes the target objective function, which may be customer specific and wherein the target objective function may include a maximization of at least one quality index of test processes, in particular, test cuts, a maximization of a feed rate of a laser head, and/or a minimization of power consumption of a laser machine (L). 11 . Method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the re-optimization algorithm (S30) continuously trains a statistical model and/or does not make use of a neural network. 12. Method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the re-optimization algorithm (S30) implements a closed loop control for automatically calculating re-optimized material-specific and/or process-specific process parameters by learning a surrogate function, based on the applied initial process parameters and the results of the evaluating (S32). 

Method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the re-optimization algorithm (S30) uses and optimizes a surrogate function to optimize the target objective function. An optimization tool (T) which is configured to execute the method according to any one of the previous method claims for calculating optimized machine- and materialspecific process parameters, being cutting parameters, for a laser cutting process to be executed on a laser machine (L), having specific machine properties, with: - A property interface (PI) for receiving determined material and/or machine properties for which the process parameters should be optimized; - A parameter interface (PARI) for receiving preconfigured initial process parameters; - A processor (P) for executing a re-optimization algorithm (S30) until a target objective function is minimized or maximized for calculating (S34) optimized material-specific process parameters by accessing a storage with a statistical model, wherein the processor (P) comprises: - A controller (C) for controlling (S31) execution of test processes with the determined preconfigured initial process parameters; - An evaluation module (EM) for evaluating (S32) the test processes by using a measurement-based algorithm or a preference-based algorithm for determining preferences for the test processes; - wherein the processor (P) is adapted to re-define (S33) a parameter domain for the re-optimization algorithm based on the determined preferences of the evaluation module (EM). A computer program comprising a computer program code, the computer program code when executed by a processor causing the optimization tool (T) to perform the steps of the method of any of the method claims.
